metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit task (or AC on the label-deployment story) to capture and commit the pre-cutover `GET /projects/rebar/access` snapshot (e.g. `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json`) before pushing the Autosubmit label, so the ADR-0042 security diff is performable.
findings.finding: The security-invariants AC requires 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot,' but the plan never tasks anyone with capturing the pre-cutover snapshot before the label is deployed, nor where it is stored. Without this artifact existing before cutover, the diff check cannot be performed.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Security-invariants artifact bullet: 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: In the label-deployment AC, prepend: 'Before pushing the label, save `curl .../projects/rebar/access` output to `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` and commit it; ADR-0042 security section diffs the post-deployment response against this file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S1 AC5 an explicit requirement to capture a `GET /projects/rebar/access` response diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot and store it as part of the security-invariants evidence, not just the label endpoint check.
findings.finding: The parent's Phase 1 AC8 (Security-invariants artifact) requires 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' as a concrete per-invariant check. S1's AC (Security-invariants) specifies a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config` and a test asserting re-verify logic, and label verification via `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` — but does NOT specify the access diff check against a pre-cutover snapshot. No other sibling covers this specific check.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC5 (Security-invariants artifact); parent Phase 1 AC8
findings.suggested_fix: In S1 AC5 (Security-invariants artifact), add: 'Capture and record a diff of GET /projects/rebar/access before and after the cutover, confirming the only new line is label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors on refs/heads/* and no broader grant was introduced.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Gerrit API field name in the serial auto-lander AC from `on_behalf_of_uploader` to `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (the actual Gerrit REST `RebaseInput` field name confirmed during remediation) so the plan body matches the verified API surface.
findings.finding: The plan's AC text asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader` as the Gerrit REST API field for rebase impersonation, but the correct field name in the Gerrit REST API is `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`. The remediation comment trail explicitly identifies and corrects this ("the RebaseInput field is `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`, not `on_behalf_of_uploader`"), but the correction was never propagated back into the epic plan body, leaving implementers who read the plan — not the comment thread — with the wrong parameter name.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: '`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `on_behalf_of_uploader` with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` in the AC bullet: '`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader: true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the outcome-provenance model: clarify whether `merged` and `review_failed` are (a) derived live by the land command from Gerrit primitives, (b) written into bot state by the lander and read by the land command, or (c) a hybrid — and update the AC 'single source of truth' claim to match exactly which outcomes flow through the bot's state store vs. are derived on-demand.
findings.finding: The Context section states 'Outcome provenance: the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3); `merged`/`review_failed` are *derived* from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)' — meaning the land command derives two outcomes independently of the bot's state store. The AC contradicts this by declaring 'The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome.' These are two different architectures: one where all 4 outcomes flow through the bot's state (S3), and one where 2 are short-circuited through the land command (S4). An implementer reading both sections could build either.
findings.location: Context § 'Agent contract = one command → one typed outcome' (Outcome provenance note) vs AC Phase 1 'Agent landing contract' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence clarification in the AC: e.g. 'The bot is the authoritative recorder for `needs_rebase` and `ci_failed`; `merged` and `review_failed` are derived by the land command from native Gerrit state (label votes / change status) at query time — together these constitute the single source of truth for the outcome enum.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the outcome provenance model: either (a) define all 4 outcomes as bot-state-recorded (making it truly a single source of truth and updating the Context note), or (b) explicitly endorse the split model and remove the 'single source of truth' language from the AC, replacing it with a precise statement of which outcomes are live-derived vs. state-read.
findings.finding: The outcome provenance model is architecturally contradictory between two sections. The Context states 'the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3); `merged`/`review_failed` are *derived* from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)' — a split-truth model. But the AC for the agent landing contract declares 'The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome.' These describe different architectures and the decision rule for which outcomes go where is not co-located with the outcome-record schema.
findings.location: Context § 'Agent contract = one command → one typed outcome' (Outcome provenance note) vs. AC Phase 1 § 'Agent landing contract' bullet ('The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome')

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or qualify all S9 references in S3's outcome record section and marker key doc, replacing with 'S4 only (S9 is archived/deferred)'.
findings.finding: S3's outcome record section and marker key spec reference S9 (batch failure) as a consumer of the S3 cache — but S9 is explicitly archived in the parent plan (S7-S9 archived; throughput deferred to idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27). The `stack_id` doc says 'the shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9' and the last sentence says 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome.'
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) — Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: In S3's Outcome record section, change 'the shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9' to 'the shared key across S2/S3/S4' and change 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache' to 'S4 (land command) consumes this cache; S9 is archived (deferred to idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the epic to reflect its committed scope (serial lander only), e.g. 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial lander (project infrastructure)', and note batching deferral in the subtitle or abstract rather than the title.
findings.finding: The plan title promises 'serial->batching' as if both phases are committed deliverables, but the body explicitly defers Phase 2 (batching) to a separate idea and archives the batching stories — creating a mismatch that will mislead agents or readers who pick up the epic from its title.
findings.location: Plan title: 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial->batching (project infrastructure)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial lander (project infrastructure; batching deferred to idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `rechecking` flag as an explicit field in S2b's wipChain schema/state-machine spec, with its lifecycle (set on recheck post, cleared on terminal vote), so S3 and S5d both bind to the same contract.
findings.finding: S3 adds a `rechecking` in-flight sub-state flag to `wipChain` (owned by S2), but S2's acceptance criteria and design do not specify the flag's interface, lifecycle, or handoff contract. S2's ACs decompose into S2a/S2b/S2c with no mention of this flag slot. This creates an implicit shared-state coordination point with no specified contract between the two stories.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) — AC1 (CI-flake auto-recheck); child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2) — S2b AC (wipChain state machine)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `rechecking: bool` field to S2b's wipChain state schema (alongside the phase enum). S3 owns setting/clearing it; S5d's crash-recovery reads it. Document the invariant: if rechecking=true and wipChain phase is awaiting-verified, the bot is mid-recheck and must not re-post the comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default polling interval and maximum wait timeout for the 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' step (e.g. poll every 30 s, abandon after 2 h) so the implementing agent has a concrete default to act on.
findings.finding: The 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' step specifies no polling interval or timeout. An implementing agent must choose these values without guidance, creating a real operational gap — too short a timeout aborts valid slow CI; too long ties up the serial lander unnecessarily.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'awaits a fresh Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the serial lander AC: 'Polls Verified status every 30 s; abandons with `ci_failed` outcome after 2 h (configurable via env var `AUTOLANDER_CI_TIMEOUT_S`, default 7200).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe the 'redundant CI runs eliminated' success criterion as the structural property that proves it in-session: e.g. 'the lander's single `wipChain` mutex ensures at most one chain is actively rebasing/awaiting CI at any time (verified by a unit/integration test or by inspection of the serialization design).'
findings.finding: The Success Criterion 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated' is not measurable in-session — it is an empirical claim about concurrent agent behavior under load, not an observable property of the deployed artifacts.
findings.location: Success Criteria section — 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand the serial-lander AC) requiring that all rebase API calls include `on_behalf_of_uploader` so the original uploader identity, DCO sign-offs, and `rebar-ticket` trailers are preserved on the new patchset — and include a verifiable check (e.g. an integration test asserting the rebase patchset uploader matches the original author).
findings.finding: The Context explicitly names `on_behalf_of_uploader` as a required rebase parameter ('rebases to the current tip … `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers') but no AC verifies that the rebase is performed with this flag. An implementer could pass all ACs while silently rebasing as the bot's own identity, stripping DCO sign-offs and `rebar-ticket` trailers.
findings.location: Context § 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet vs AC Phase 1 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the serial-lander AC bullet: '… all rebase calls set `on_behalf_of_uploader` (preserving original uploader identity, DCO sign-offs, and `rebar-ticket` trailers); a test asserts the rebased patchset's uploader equals the original author.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure for the `refs/meta/config` push (e.g., 'revert commit on refs/meta/config and re-push to restore pre-cutover ACL snapshot') in the Phase 1 AC or a dedicated rollout section.
findings.finding: The plan requires a push to `refs/meta/config` (live Gerrit project config) to introduce the `Autosubmit` label, but provides no explicit rollback/back-out path if the label config causes problems post-push. T4 requires that irreversible/destructive steps carry an explicit remediation — acknowledgement alone is insufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1, first AC bullet (`Autosubmit` Gerrit label authored in `infra/gerrit/project.config` / pushed to `refs/meta/config`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line AC or a rollout note: 'Back-out: revert the `refs/meta/config` commit and push to restore the pre-cutover snapshot captured in the Security invariants diff; verify with `GET /projects/rebar/access`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure the rewritten `CLAUDE.md`/`CONTRIBUTING.md` retains (or explicitly links to) a fallback manual-submit procedure for use when the autolander is unavailable.
findings.finding: The plan rewrites `CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` to the new 'set Autosubmit and walk away' contract but provides no fallback procedure (or pointer to one) for when the autolander service is unavailable. If the service is down and docs no longer describe the manual rebase-and-submit path, agents are left without a recovery path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1, Docs bullet ('rewrite this repo's landing section')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Service unavailable fallback' subsection in the landing docs describing manual rebase-and-submit steps, or preserve the existing manual workflow section under a clearly labeled fallback heading.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assign a documentation home (e.g., a schema subsection in ADR-0042, or a `infra/autolander/README.md`) for the outcome-record state-store schema.
findings.finding: The outcome-record schema (what the bot writes to its own state store when recording `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed`) is a new persistent data contract introduced by this epic, but the plan assigns no documentation artifact — ADR-0042's described scope ('records the auto-lander + agent contract' + Security invariants) does not explicitly include the state-store schema.
findings.location: Context section ('the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)') and Greenfield note ('the outcome-record schema… [is a] NEW artifact created by this epic')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'State-store schema' subsection to ADR-0042 (or a separate schema file in `infra/autolander/`) that defines the fields, types, and versioning strategy for outcome records.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a probe or integration-test fixture (even a single Gerrit API call against a test repo or sandbox) that verifies `on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase preserves DCO and `rebar-ticket` trailers before the serial lander is declared production-ready.
findings.finding: The `on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase preserving DCO and `rebar-ticket` trailers is asserted as a known behavior but no probe, spike, or test fixture is specified to validate this specific Gerrit API integration point before full build-out.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'on_behalf_of_uploader to preserve author/DCO/rebar-ticket trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or story task: 'Run a manual probe against the project's Gerrit instance (or a clone) using POST /changes/{id}/rebase with on_behalf_of_uploader; assert that Signed-off-by and rebar-ticket trailers appear unchanged in the resulting patchset commit message. Capture the result as a fixture or CI integration test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (in the plan, ADR-0042, or a dedicated AC) the interface contract between the `land`/`land-status` command and the auto-lander bot — at minimum: the communication mechanism (e.g., S3 key schema + polling, or HTTP endpoint), the outcome-record schema, and which component is authoritative for each outcome type.
findings.finding: The communication interface between the `land`/`land-status` project command and the auto-lander service (wipChain bot) is unspecified — the plan introduces a cross-component dependency without defining the boundary (HTTP API, S3 polling, shared queue, etc.).
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Agent landing contract' bullet and Context 'Outcome provenance' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ADR-0042's scope: 'Interface contract: the land command polls S3 key `autolander/outcomes/{change_id}.json` (schema: {outcome: merged|needs_rebase|ci_failed|review_failed, sha: string, ts: ISO8601}) written by the bot; merged/review_failed are derived by the command from Gerrit REST, not from S3.' Adjust schema as appropriate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete validation mechanism for the 'redundant CI runs eliminated' criterion — e.g., an integration test or post-cutover metric target (e.g., 'a two-agent race on a non-conflicting stack produces exactly N CI runs, not 2N'), or a dogfooding note specifying how the team will observe the improvement after rollout.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism exists for the 'redundant CI runs eliminated' success criterion — there is no before/after baseline, metric target, or dogfooding step that would let an agent confirm this outcome was actually achieved.
findings.location: Success Criteria section: 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider merging S2 (core loop) and S3 (failure handling) into a single story — the happy path and failure paths of the lander loop are one algorithmic concern; S3 alone is not independently deployable or valuable, making this a horizontal code-path split rather than a coherent vertical slice.
findings.finding: S2 (core loop: happy path rebase→verify→submit) and S3 (failure handling: 409/CI-fail, stack hand-back) split the SAME algorithmic concern by code-path. S3 alone delivers nothing independently deployable or testable — the failure paths only exist in the context of the running lander loop. This is a horizontal layer-style split within one concern, not two independently-releasable outcomes.
findings.location: Child stories: perovskite-frilly-grebe (S2) and darksome-starry-sable (S3)
findings.suggested_fix: Collapse S2 + S3 into one story ('Serial auto-lander core loop incl. failure handling: rebase:chain → await Verified → ancestor-atomic submit; on 409/CI-fail remove Autosubmit and record typed outcome; never partial-land'). The combined story remains a single session's work given the algorithm is small and already fully specified in the epic AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that for relation chains the lander must await Verified +1 on ALL N chain members (not just the tip) before submitting, and define the outcome mapping when a mid-chain change fails CI (e.g., `ci_failed` for the whole stack, whole stack handed back).
findings.finding: For a relation chain of N changes, a chain-rebase (`POST rebase:chain`) mints N new patchsets. Under the strict `Verified` copyCondition (`NO_CODE_CHANGE` only, explicitly excluding `TRIVIAL_REBASE`), all N changes have Verified dropped and each gets its own independent CI run. The plan's 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' is singular — an implementer will likely watch only the HEAD/tip change's Verified status. If the lander calls submit while inner chain members still have `Verified=0` (their CI is still running), Gerrit's submit requirement (`label:Verified=MAX` on every change in the chain) will block the submit with an error that is neither a `needs_rebase` (no 409/FFO conflict) nor a `ci_failed` (CI hasn't concluded yet) — it is an 'awaiting CI on chain members' state the plan's failure model does not cover. The lander has no defined recovery for this case.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'awaits a fresh Verified +1 → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the serial lander AC to read: 'For a relation chain of N changes, awaits fresh `Verified +1` on EVERY change in the chain (not just the tip) before submitting — since chain-rebase mints N new patchsets each requiring their own CI run under the strict `NO_CODE_CHANGE` copyCondition. A `Verified -1` on any chain member maps to `ci_failed` for the whole stack.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the queue-ordering policy for selecting 'the front Autosubmit change/chain' when multiple Autosubmit-labeled changes are queued (e.g., ascending Gerrit change number, or earliest Autosubmit vote timestamp, or tip-ancestry order).
findings.finding: The plan states the serial lander processes 'the front Autosubmit change/chain' and uses a 'single wipChain (serialized, one instance),' but gives no mechanism for how the lander selects WHICH change/chain is 'front' when multiple independent agents have each set Autosubmit on different changes concurrently. Without a stated ordering policy (e.g., FIFO by Autosubmit vote timestamp, by change number, by uploader, or by tip-ancestry position), two different implementations of the lander could produce different queue orderings — breaking the serial invariant or causing starvation of some agents' changes.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'for the front Autosubmit change/chain it rebases…'; Context: 'Single `wipChain` (serialized, one instance)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the serial lander AC: 'When multiple changes have Autosubmit set, the lander selects the front by ascending Gerrit change number (a stable proxy for submission order) — the lowest-numbered Autosubmit+submittable chain is processed first.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit SSM SecureString slot for the autolander's Gerrit service-account credential (e.g. `/rebar/prod/gerrit-autolander-token`) in `ssm.tf` and document whether an `iam_autolander.tf` inline-policy extension is needed on the instance role, consistent with the project's single-owner contract.
findings.finding: The autolander service needs a Gerrit service-account credential to call the rebase and submit REST APIs, but the plan specifies no new SSM SecureString slot for it — it is silent on whether a new bot token is provisioned (requiring a new ssm.tf entry + fetch-secrets.sh mapping) or whether the existing `gerrit-bot-token` is reused. Reusing the review-bot's credential violates least privilege; a new credential needs an explicit IaC declaration consistent with the project's single-owner pattern.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/gerrit-autolander-token` to `local.rebar_secret_params` in `ssm.tf` and add an `iam_autolander.tf` documentation-anchor file (mirroring `iam_s2.tf`/`iam_s4a.tf`) stating whether the existing `/rebar/prod/*` SSM-read grant already covers it or a new scoped policy is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the autolander's wipChain/outcome-record persistence medium in the IaC deliverables (e.g., a new `gerrit_autolander` external volume declaration in `docker-compose.yml` backed by EBS, mirroring the `gerrit_reviewbot` pattern), or explicitly state it is ephemeral and document the implications for in-flight jobs across restarts.
findings.finding: The autolander service's 'wipChain' and outcome-record state storage medium is left unspecified in the plan's IaC deliverables — the phrase 'its own state (S3)' is ambiguous between story S3 and AWS S3 object storage. If the state is persisted on the EBS-backed host volume, a new bind-mount path and external volume declaration (following the gerrit_reviewbot pattern in docker-compose.yml) is needed but not called out; if it is ephemeral, that is a correctness problem (state lost on container restart).
findings.location: Context section — 'Outcome provenance: the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `gerrit_autolander` external named volume in `docker-compose.yml` (bind-mounted from `/var/gerrit/site/autolander` on the EBS host path, created by `compose-up.sh` alongside the other external volumes), and document this in the autolander container's service definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `monitoring_autolander.tf` CloudWatch alarm (or a named alarm in an existing monitoring file) that watches a structured autolander failure log marker, mirroring the `voter_errors` pattern in `monitoring_s4b.tf`, to the plan's IaC deliverables.
findings.finding: The plan routes autolander logs to container stdout / CloudWatch but specifies no new CloudWatch alarm for the autolander service's own health (e.g., process crash, repeated failed-land loop, stalled wipChain). Every other service-level failure mode has a dedicated alarm (reviewer errors → `monitoring_s4b.tf`; gate-down → `monitoring.tf`; replication errors → `monitoring_s5.tf`), yet the autolander — whose failure silently orphans Autosubmit labels — has no monitoring IaC called out.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Observability: the bot logs to its own service logs (container stdout → existing CloudWatch monitoring)'
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `rebar-autolander-errors` CloudWatch alarm in `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf` watching a custom metric (e.g., `rebar/host:autolander_errors`) published by `observability.sh` grepping for a structured `LANDER_ERROR` log marker in the autolander container's journald output — exactly the pattern used in `monitoring_s4b.tf`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `wipChain` S3 state is idempotent/resumable: define the step-level state machine (e.g., `REBASED`, `AWAITING_VERIFIED`, `SUBMITTING`) so that a restarted service re-enters at the correct step and never double-applies the rebase or submits a stale SHA.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a serialized S3-backed `wipChain` state but does not specify whether it is resumable/idempotent on service restart. If the autolander crashes between the rebase step and the submit step, the wipChain record in S3 may reflect an in-progress state; the plan gives no indication that re-starting the service will correctly resume (re-verify submittability, re-check Verified, re-submit) rather than double-applying the rebase or submitting a stale SHA.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet; Context — 'wipChain (serialized, one instance)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `wipChain` state-machine spec to the relevant child story (S-lander): states = `{IDLE, REBASING, AWAITING_VERIFIED, SUBMITTING, DONE}`; on restart, read current state and branch — if `REBASING`, check whether the rebase already completed (query Gerrit current revision); if `AWAITING_VERIFIED`, re-poll; if `SUBMITTING`, re-verify submittability then re-submit. All transitions must be idempotent (Gerrit rebase is a no-op if already at tip).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a rollback strategy for S3 outcome-record state: at minimum, specify a schema version field in every record and that the `land-status` command treats unrecognised versions as `unknown` (rather than crashing), so rollback cannot cause silent data loss.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new S3 outcome-record schema ('the outcome-record schema' is listed as a new artifact) but specifies no rollback path for S3 state if the autolander service must be rolled back to a prior version after outcome records have been written in a new format. A rolled-back service reading unrecognised S3 records could silently drop outcomes or crash, causing data loss from the agent's perspective.
findings.location: Context — 'Outcome provenance: the bot records needs_rebase/ci_failed in its own state (S3)'; Greenfield note — 'outcome-record schema … [are] NEW artifacts'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike or pre-implementation probe (e.g., a `curl` against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API) to confirm the exact paths `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`, `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain`, and the `on_behalf_of_uploader` RebaseInput field name before committing the implementation story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `on_behalf_of_uploader` body field and `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` (chain rebase) as the core lander mechanism, but no probe, spike, or reference confirms these specific endpoint paths and parameter names exist on the deployed Gerrit 3.14.1 instance — this is a new REST-API surface not used anywhere in the current codebase.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'rebases to the current tip (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-story spike (or expand the S1/lead story) with: `curl -u bot:token -X POST https://<gerrit>/a/changes/<id>/rebase -d '{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}` and the chain variant, confirming 200 vs 404 and the exact response shape on Gerrit 3.14.1. Record the verified endpoint surfaces in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Gerrit REST endpoint and parameters used to re-verify submittability on the exact tested SHA (e.g., `GET /changes/{id}?o=SUBMITTABLE` plus current-revision SHA comparison), and confirm these exist on Gerrit 3.14.1 before the implementation story.
findings.finding: The plan states the autolander 're-verify `is:submittable` on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' as a security invariant, but does not name or verify the specific Gerrit REST endpoint/query parameter used to perform this check — a new integration surface for this project.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Security-invariants artifact bullet: 're-verify `is:submittable` on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the authentication scheme for the autolander's Gerrit REST calls (credential type, identity/service-account, and SSM parameter name) in the plan's serial auto-lander AC or a dedicated auth preconditions note.
findings.finding: The plan does not state auth/HTTPS preconditions for the autolander service's outbound REST calls to Gerrit (credential type, identity, and whether HTTP Digest / Bearer token / HTTP password is used), leaving a gap for implementors.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet; Context — 'the bot casts no votes; the grant is tightly scoped'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the auto-lander's Gerrit service-account identity (new dedicated account vs. shared bot identity), its SSM parameter slot (e.g. `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token`), the minimum-required ACL (submit + rebase on behalf of uploader only), and how `fetch-secrets.sh` / the compose environment materialises the credential at boot — consistent with ADR-0008.
findings.finding: The auto-lander service makes authenticated Gerrit REST API calls (rebase, submit) across the public-internet-reachable HTTPS boundary, but the plan never declares the lander's own Gerrit service-account identity, its SSM parameter slot, or how the credential is fetched/materialised at runtime — leaving the new boundary-crossing component with an undeclared access posture.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet; Context 'the bot records … in its own state (S3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated Gerrit service account for the autolander (separate from the review-bot to limit blast radius), store its HTTP token at `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` (SSM SecureString), extend `fetch-secrets.sh` to materialise it as `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` in `.env`, and grant it only `label-Autosubmit = 0..0` (no vote needed), `submit` on `refs/heads/*`, and `rebase` — nothing more. Document this in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section alongside the other per-invariant checks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit outcome-record schema (field names, types, required vs. optional) to the plan — e.g. `{change_id, stack_ids[], outcome, recorded_at, error_detail?}` — so S3 and S4 stories implement against the same contract.
findings.finding: The plan names 'the outcome-record schema' as a new greenfield artifact the epic creates (Greenfield note) and the bot 'records' `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3), but the outcome-record schema's field vocabulary — e.g. which fields it carries, their types, and their cardinality — is never defined anywhere in the plan. An implementer of the S3 state store and the S4 land command cannot write a conformant record without inventing the schema.
findings.location: Context § 'Outcome provenance' note; Greenfield note ('the outcome-record schema…are all NEW artifacts created by this epic's stories')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a **Outcome-record schema** subsection (e.g. under AC Phase 1 or as a named appendix) with at minimum: `change_id` (Gerrit change number of the stack head), `stack_change_ids` (ordered list), `outcome` (enum: `needs_rebase|ci_failed`), `recorded_at` (ISO-8601), and optional `error_detail` (string). Reference this schema in both the S3 failure-handling AC and the S4 land-command AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for a failed `remove Autosubmit` operation in the failure-handling AC: at minimum, clarify whether the removal should be retried, treated as idempotent (404 = success), or escalated to a service-log alert if it fails after N retries.
findings.finding: The failure-handling sub-path for the `remove Autosubmit` operation itself has no specified fallback. The plan says on CI-fail or 409 the lander 'remove[s] `Autosubmit`' — but if that removal fails (Gerrit unreachable, permission error, or the label was already removed by the author), the outcome is undefined: the change may remain in a pseudo-queued state with `Autosubmit` set but no active lander processing it, permanently blocking the queue.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 § 'Failure handling' bullet: 'CI-fail → remove `Autosubmit`…; rebase-conflict (`409`) → remove `Autosubmit` and record a conflict outcome.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the failure-handling AC bullet: 'Label removal is idempotent — a 404 (already absent) is treated as success; a non-404 error is retried up to 3× with exponential backoff; persistent failure is logged to service logs as a WARNING and the change is skipped from the wipChain until the next poll cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify ownership: move 'a test asserting the re-verify logic' from S1's AC5 to S2b's AC (where the TOCTOU guard logic lives), and add a cross-reference in S1's AC5 pointing to S2b's test as the concrete check to cite in ADR-0042.
findings.finding: S1 (843b) commits to 'a test asserting the re-verify logic' as part of its Security-invariants artifact, but the re-verify logic (checking `is:submittable` on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit) is implemented in S2c/S2b, not in S1. S1 has no implementation scope that would produce or house this test. S6 owns ADR-0042 where the invariant is recorded, but the test itself must live in S2b/S2c — which are sub-children of S2 and are not listed as dependents of S1.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC5; child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2) — AC (Land the tested tree, S2c)
findings.suggested_fix: In S1 AC5, replace 'a test asserting the re-verify logic' with 'a cross-reference to S2b's TOCTOU guard test (which asserts the re-verify invariant)'. In S2b's AC, add an explicit requirement: 'The TOCTOU guard test is annotated as the security-invariants concrete check for the re-verify-before-submit invariant, to be cited in ADR-0042.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S6's dependency list: 'Also depends on S1 AC5 (security-invariants content) being finalized before ADR-0042's security invariants section is authored — not just the label being live.'
findings.finding: S6 (b825) lists S1 and S4 as explicit dependencies ('Docs must not merge to main until the label and command they instruct agents to use are actually live'), but S6's ADR-0042 must also incorporate the Security invariants content that S1 (843b) authors (S1 AC5 references ADR-0042). This content-readiness dependency on S1's security invariants section is not captured in S6's dependency list — only the 'label and command are live' dependency is stated.
findings.location: child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) — Dependencies section; child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC5
findings.suggested_fix: In S6's Dependencies section, add: 'Content dependency on S1 AC5: the ADR-0042 Security invariants section must incorporate S1's finalized concrete checks (grep proof, access diff, re-verify test reference) and cannot be drafted until S1 AC5 content is settled.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
